method: margin>=t_high->AI; margin<t_low->human; ambiguous band decided by LogisticRegression on orthogonal handcrafted features (ttr, repeated_bigram_ratio, repeated_trigram_ratio, avg_word_length, text_length, punctuation_per_token)
leakage_statement: P0/P2 use test labels for calibration -> in-distribution ceiling, NOT a test score. P1 is the only legal test score (all params fit on dev, test predicted once).

P2_nested_cv_upper_bound.note: band selected on inner calib split, no optimism; in-dist calib ceiling

P1_deploy_dev_to_test_once.note: ONLY legal test score; dev=bloomz+human, full distribution shift vs test

key_findings: P0 vs P2 differ by only 0.0016 -> band-selection is not overfitting; method is genuinely effective
P1 region-aware 0.857 > old single-model SOTA Fusion-LGBM 0.835 (which was test-tuned)
P1-vs-P2 gap 0.088 = cost of dev not representing test (distribution shift)
single global threshold ceiling 0.886 was an artifact of single-threshold + class imbalance, NOT a data limit
